title: Create subset projects from community picture dictionaries
notes: Created from human suggestion #27 (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-06-13). Sophie requested a way for community organisers to create named project-like subsets from a full picture dictionary, initially by manual word/page selection and optionally with an AI-proposed candidate subset from a natural-language description followed by human adjustment. The subset should remain derived from the main picture dictionary: it should inherit page/image content from the canonical dictionary, automatically reflect later main-dictionary image/content updates where possible, and should not expose the normal organiser image-review/regeneration mechanisms directly on the subset because that could create conflicting image curation states. Track UX, data model, provenance, update semantics, naming, edit/retrieve flows, and exercise-source integration for flashcards, word scrambles, crosswords, and related picture-clue activities. Initial roadmap details are in docs/roadmap/picture-dictionaries.md. Priority was raised to P1 on 2026-06-13 after Sophie reported that subset dictionary projects are integral to the first round of real classroom tests beginning around 2026-07-13. The first implementation cut should prioritise manual named subset creation/retrieval/editing from canonical dictionary entries, with canonical-image inheritance and no direct image-review/regeneration surface on subset projects; AI-proposed subset selection can follow once the manual flow is usable. Follow-up on 2026-06-13 added an AI-assisted subset prefill control: organisers can enter a subset description, the system evaluates candidate entries primarily through their gloss/translation values, and the resulting checked entries remain editable before save. As of 2026-06-13, the implemented flow has been exercised successfully: description-based AI prefill, subset creation, and flashcard exercise generation from the resulting subdictionary all functioned as intended. Next step is server deployment on 2026-06-14 and Sophie review; keep the issue active/P1 until that user review confirms the workflow is classroom-ready.
